Fern Polrrrcar- PRACTTcEs C orraMrssroN
-128 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramenro. CA 95gl_l-23:9
(916) -322-5660 o Fax (916) 322_0886
January 25,2008
VIA FACSIMILE and U.S. MAIL
Katrina Anne Foley
Lents & Foley LLP
1600 Dove Street, Suite l0l
Newport Beach, CA92660
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-07-199
Dear Ms. Foley:
This letter responds to your reqllest for advice on behalf of Garden Grove unified
School District board member Dr. Kimoanh Nguyen-Lam regarding the gift provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 The advice provided is baJed on-the ru""r, pr"r"nted; the
Fair Political Practices commission (the "commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it
renders advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) I FppC Ops. 71.)
Please note that the Commission will not advise with respect to past conduct.
(Regulation 18329(bX8XA).) Therefore, nothing in this letter should be constmed to evaluate
any conduct that may have already taken place, and any conclusions contained in this letter
apply only to prospective actions and future filing obligations. Therefore, any action taken by
your client prior to receipt of this formal advice has no-immunity.
In your letter. you seek written confirmation of oral advice received from this office.
However' the commission does not confirm telephone advice.2 rrris rltter *iii p.ouioe formal
advice based on the facts set forth in your letter of Decemb er 20,2007.
rThe Political Reform Act is contained in Government code Sections glOfi) through gl0l4. All
statutory references are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulationl'of the Fair
RrliticalPracticesCommissionarecontainedinSections lSll0throu!hlggg1,fritle2of theCalitbrnia
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2. Divisiori6 of the California C,de of
Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.
I relephone advice by its very nature is infirrmal and is based on infrrrmation and research which
must be gathered in a fairly short period of time. For this reason we do nut "conflrm" telephone advice by
way of a tormal' written reply' However. we will provide firrmal advise with respect tn prlrrf..:ti'e actiors
when it is specifically requested. With this pr,,c.rr. there is no guarantee that the ttrrmal advice will match
the prior intbrmal assistance.


File No. A-07-199
Page No. 2
QUESTTON
May donors pay legal fees as a gift to Ms. Nguyen-Lam, where the lawsuit is unrelated
to and does not arise directly out of her activities, duties, and status as a candidate?
CONCLUSION
Yes, donors may make payments for legal fees owed by Ms. Nguyen-Lam. However,
because the legal fees do not relate to her candidacy or elective office,iayments for them are
gifts to Ms' Nguyen-Lam and subject to the following requirements and rlstrictions: (l) If the
donors are reportable on Ms. Nguyen-Lam's Stateme-nt oi E.onornic Interests (Form 700), she
must report gifts from each donor that total to $50 or more during the applicable reporting'
period and she cannot accept gifts from the same donors that total to over $390 in a calendar
yeat; (2) Ms' Nguyen-Lam may not make, participate in making or use her official position to
influence a governmental decision that wilihave a material financial effect on any donor
whose gifts totaled to $390 or more during the l2-month period preceding the govemmental
decision.
FACTS
Your firm represents board member Nguyen-Lam, who filed a personal civil lawsuit
against another school district that is unrelated to her position as an elected school board
member with the Garden Grove Unified School District. Ms. Nguyen-Lam utr".a to u
settlement of attorney's fees to one of the defendants in the lawsuit.
You seek formal written advice regarding: (l) whether donors may pay legal fees
related to the lawsuit, (2) whether these payments constitute gifts to rrr.. Njry"n-i"-;;una fll
if the payments are gifts, what are Ms. Nguyen-Lam's obligations and restrictions under the
Act as the result of receiving the gifts.
In our January 22,2008 telephone conversation, you stated that another attorney, acting
on behalf of Ms' Nguyen-Lam, collected checks (made directly payable to the creditor) of up to
$390.00 from individuals, corporations, and political action ";;ir',.";.ifi; 
lr,o."y
forwarded these checks to your law firm, and you, firm, on behalf of Ms. Nguy"r-r-om, sent
the checks to the creditor's attomeys in order io help pay for her legal oUtigitions.
I In your letter. you state that your oftice sought telephone advice about this issue from the FppC
on November 28,2N7, and December 10.20o7, and your office and Ms. Nguyen-Lam acted-based on the
telephone advice received.
a we are limiting our advice to your sp.::ifi: question dealing with reporting and other obligations
of Ms' Nguyen-Lam. the recipient of the gifls. we do not address aniobligations or-restrictinns trrat ,ay
be applicable to the provider of the gifts, nor do w-e offer any opinion on thi legality o, opprnfrioreness of
the political action committees providing these gifts.


File No. A-07-199
Page No. 3
You understand that Ms. Nguyen-Lam is required to report gifts on her Form 700, but
you also wish to know if there are disclosure (otherihan reporting the gifts on her Form 700)
and other requirements that Ms. Nguyen-Lam must follow.
AI\ALYSIS
C o nt ri b u ti ons and G if-t s
when a payment is made to a public official and the public official does not provide
something of equal value.in ex_change, the paymen, rnuy be either a political contribution or a
gift to that official. (Sections g2015 anO SiO2S.)
Such a payment is a contribution if it is made for a political puqpose. (Section g2015;
Regulation 18225.\ otherwise, the payment is normally considereoto Le a girt. (Section
82028.)
The Act defines a "contribution" as a "payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a
PT uv a third party, or an enforceable promisi to make a payment except to the extent that
full and adequate consideration is received, unless it is cleir from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political purposes." (Section g2015.)
A "gift" is defined under the Act as_"any payment that confers a personal benefit on the
recipient, to the extent that consideration or equai oi greater value is not received and includes
a rebate or discount} ,h: price of anything of value Inless the rebate or discount is made in
the regular course of business to memberr of th" public without regard to oiri"iur status...,,
(Section 82028.)
- I! Ms' Nguyen-Lam receives funds to defray legal costs incurred in a personal lawsuit
unrelated to her current position as a school board ..rib.., we would not consider these
payments to be a political contribution. 
-lnstead, they would be gifts ur trr"y.onfer a personal
benefit to her, and they must be reported accordingry on h., Form 700.
Gtfts - Wo is the Recipient?
Regulation 18941provides that the recipient of a gift is the person who actually
receives the gift or the person who directs ttre gift. s 
"
According to y.ou: facts, Ms. Nguyen-Lam is the recipient of the gifts because an
attorney acting on her behalf collected the checks, and your law firm ""riig ". 
t er agent,
5 where gitls are given to an official (or an official's agent) with the understanding that rhe gifrs
will be distributed to others at the otficial's discretion. ttre girts ire ieemed to be gifts to the public otrcial
(white Advice Letter, No. I-92-2ltt.) ln short. any 1en t1a! Ms. Nguyen-Lam may take in rouring the gift
towards any party is an exercise of "control" under Regulation taqTl'that would make her a recipient of
the gifts.


File No. A-07-199
Page No. 4
forwarded the checks to the defendant's law firm to pay off attorney's fees she owes pursuant
to the settlement.6
Obligations Triggered by Receipt of Gifts:
The receipt of gifts triggers several obligations for public officials under the Act:
Conflict-of-Intergst:- Gifts aggregating of $390 or more from a single source provided
to, received by, or promised to a public orriciut within 12 months prior to ihe time when a
decision is made may create a conflict of interest for a state or local official and require the
official to disqualify himself or herself from participatint in the governmental decisions that
financially affect the donor of the gift. (Section gzio:(ej.l
Disclosure: A public official must disclose gift totaling $50 or more from a single
source in a calendar year if that source is required to-be reported on the official,s Statement of
Economic Interests. (Section 97207.)
Gift Limits: Th9 Act provides gift limits with regard to cerrain public officials. Section
89503 provides that no local elected officer may-accept Eirtr in excess or $rqo from a single
source in a calendar year. If the local elected oifi.". ir Ja"rig.rur"J ".rprrv.y "r 
his or her
agency, the $390 annual gift limit applies only to gifts from donors wtro mrst be reported on
the official' s Statement of Economit-Interests. (section g9503(c). )
Therefore, because Ms. Nguyen-Lam has received reportable gifts, she is subject to the
above rules. She must report gifti fiom reportable donors on h", noni 700. In addition, she
cannot take gifts from these donors in excess of the $390 annual limit, and must not participate
in any decision that will materially affect the donors of gifis rotaling $390 or more.
If you have other questions on this matter, please conract me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
By:
Counsel
Commission Counsel
Legal Division
6 Even if donors send the checks directly to the defendant's raw firm to offset legal costs Ms.
Nguyen-Lam owes, the payments are gifts to her. This is consistent *itr, or. previous advice. we have
advised that a candidate is deemed to have received u cont.iurtion-irite-.rnaiaate,s obligations for legal
fees are reduced as a result ofpayments from a donor, even ifthe.unaiaor. did not actualty receive the
payment himself' (Robeni Advice Letter. No. I-91-292.) Because thase payments are being used to reduce
Ms' Nguyen-Lam's legal fees. they confer a personal benefit to t., unJ, accordingly. are gifis to her even if
she does not directly receive the payment.
' Section 8201 9 detlnes "designated employee" to include any officer or employee of an agency
who holds elective office, other than elective state office.

to and does not arise directly out of her activities, duties, and status as a candidate?

CONCLUSION: Yes, donors may make payments for legal fees owed by Ms. Nguyen-Lam. However,

whose gifts totaled to $390 or more during the l2-month period preceding the govemmental
decision.
